source_old_req: The alarmAggregator thread shall process input error data from the arlarm_acc_in and arlarm_adirup_in ports, and produce a combined error output on the alarmAggregator_out port. The thread shall execute periodically with a period of 3000 ms, have a deadline of 500 ms, and operate at priority level 6. The thread shall guarantee that the alarmAggregator_out port value is determined exclusively by the current values of arlarm_acc_in and arlarm_adirup_in, along with the internal computations performed by the alarmAggregator_spg subprogram. The thread shall ensure that all data written to the alarmAggregator_out port conforms to the SHM_DataType::errorData data type. The thread shall not violate the data type constraints of the arlarm_acc_in, arlarm_adirup_in, or alarmAggregator_out ports under any circumstances.
natural_requirement: The alarmAggregator component shall define the implementation-level signal routing for alarmAggregator.impl. In the implementation layer, alarmAggregator.impl shall route data flow from arlarm_acc_in to alarmAggregator.inData_para1 through C1 and data flow from arlarm_adirup_in to alarmAggregator.inData_para2 through C2.

requirement_items.text: The alarmAggregator.impl implementation shall route the data flow from arlarm_acc_in to alarmAggregator.inData_para1 through connection C1.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The alarmAggregator.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from arlarm_acc_in to alarmAggregator.inData_para1.

requirement_items.text: The alarmAggregator.impl implementation shall route the data flow from arlarm_adirup_in to alarmAggregator.inData_para2 through connection C2.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The alarmAggregator.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from arlarm_adirup_in to alarmAggregator.inData_para2.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

natural_requirement_before_behavior_styl: The alarmAggregator.impl implementation shall declare connection C1 from arlarm_acc_in to alarmAggregator.inData_para1. The alarmAggregator.impl implementation shall declare connection C2 from arlarm_adirup_in to alarmAggregator.inData_para2.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

natural_requirement_before_paragraph_rew: The alarmAggregator.impl implementation shall route the data flow from arlarm_acc_in to alarmAggregator.inData_para1 through connection C1. The alarmAggregator.impl implementation shall route the data flow from arlarm_adirup_in to alarmAggregator.inData_para2 through connection C2.

paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
